techniques.evidence_quote: five months later, in September 2018, Ramesh accessed Cisco's cloud infrastructure hosted on Amazon's Web Services
Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines
deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams
temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts
Webex Teams outage was the result of intentional sabotage

attack_chain.description: Former Cisco engineer Sudhish Kasaba Ramesh, five months after resigning, used still-valid AWS credentials to access Cisco's cloud infrastructure without authorization.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: five months later, in September 2018, Ramesh accessed Cisco's cloud infrastructure hosted on Amazon's Web Services

evidence_chunks.quote: five months later, in September 2018, Ramesh accessed Cisco's cloud infrastructure hosted on Amazon's Web Services
five months later, in September 2018, Ramesh accessed Cisco's cloud infrastructure hosted on Amazon's Web Services

attack_chain.description: Ramesh executed a script against the AWS environment to automate deletion of EC2 instances hosting WebEx Teams.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines

evidence_chunks.quote: Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines
Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines

attack_chain.description: The script deleted 456 EC2 virtual machines supporting WebEx Teams infrastructure, destroying the underlying servers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams

evidence_chunks.quote: deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams
deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams

attack_chain.description: As a downstream consequence of the VM deletions, more than 16,000 Webex user accounts were temporarily removed, denying users access to the service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: actions that resulted in the temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts

evidence_chunks.quote: actions that resulted in the temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts
actions that resulted in the temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts

initial_access_vector: Former employee used retained/valid AWS credentials five months after resignation to log in to Cisco's AWS environment without authorization.
persistence_mechanism: Credentials/access were not revoked after employee's April 2018 resignation, allowing access in September 2018.
privilege_escalation: None required — existing account retained sufficient privileges to terminate EC2 instances.
